query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research on CDC Travel Health Notices for North American countries. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Frequency/Rate:** New North America–specific notices appear roughly **once every 2–4 months (~0.3/month)**. When including periodic updates to global/regional notices that cover North American countries, the effective rate rises to **~0.5–0.7 notice events per month**. North America is a small share of CDC's total THN volume (~25 active notices at any time, several new ones per month globally, most concerning Africa/South America/Asia).

**Recent 2025–2026 North America notices with dates:**
- Diphtheria in Haiti — Level 1, **New, June 25, 2026**
- Global Dengue (updated) — Level 1, **June 23, 2026**
- Hepatitis A in Canada (Manitoba) — Level 1, **New, June 4, 2026**
- Global Polio (incl. Panama) — Level 2, **March 9, 2026**
- Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in Mexico (northern border states) — Level 1, **Dec 23, 2025**
- Oropouche in the Americas (Cuba, Panama) — Level 1, **updated Sept 18, 2025**
- Global Measles (all NA regions) — Level 1, **updated May 28, 2025**
- Early-2025 versions: Global Dengue (Feb 26, 2025), Global Measles (Feb 21, 2025), Oropouche in the Americas (Jan 31, 2025)

**Notable caveat:** Many "North American" notices are actually **global notices** (Measles, Dengue, Polio, Oropouche) that periodically list NA countries, while truly novel country-specific NA notices (Hepatitis A/Canada, Diphtheria/Haiti, RMSF/Mexico) are less frequent. The New World screwworm situation across 8 Mexico/Central American countries appears as destination-page alerts rather than a numbered standalone THN. Findings and sources have been recorded in the structured output.

Panama (March 9, 2026).

sources.title: Guatemala - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: New World screwworm myiasis reported in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, and Panama.

sources.title: New World screwworm: rare human case identified in United States after travel from El Salvador
sources.snippet: A rare case of travel-related New World screwworm identified in Maryland in a patient who traveled from El Salvador (August 2025).

sources.title: CDC Issues Urgent Advisory As Dengue Fever Cases Surge In Mexico And 19 Other Countries
sources.snippet: CDC dengue travel advisory for Mexico and 19 other countries, updated May 2025; over 13 million dengue cases reported across North, Central, and South America.
